mnemonics: FAMILY TREE: Think of a multilevel hierarchy like a family tree (Grandparent -> Parent -> Child), where each generation inherits traits from the one above.
keyPoints: A multilevel hierarchy involves a chain of inheritance, e.g., class A extends B, and class B extends C.
A subclass inherits all the non-private members (fields and methods) from all its superclasses up the chain.
You can add new fields and methods or override existing methods at any level in the hierarchy to specialize the class.
commonMistakes: Forgetting that a subclass can only directly call the constructors of its immediate superclass using super(), not any constructor further up the chain.
Creating overly deep and complex hierarchies that are difficult to maintain, when composition might be a better design choice.
